arguments.reason: purchasing replaced it with the approved SKU on inv-640

stages.purpose: Submit the authorized cancellation once. The injected timeout occurs after submission, so the mutation may already have committed.
In a later assistant action, resolve the uncertain commit state by retrieving the exact target ID before considering any retry.

state_assertions: inv-277 is cancelled with the supplied reason after the timed-out call.
inventory_cancel is invoked exactly once; verification does not trigger a second mutation.
inv-640 and inv-877 remain unchanged, and no notification is sent.
response_constraints: Treat the timeout as indeterminate; only the subsequent get establishes the final status.
After the get verifies cancelled, do not retry the cancellation.
Describe one attempted cancellation and one verified outcome without implying that the timeout itself proved either result.
Use exactly two bullets: verification result first, retry decision second.

records.name: Winter Deicer Pallet Reorder — Approved SKU

prompt: The depot buyer is removing an obsolete reorder after procurement approved a replacement SKU. Cancel “Winter Deicer Pallet Reorder” (inv-277) because purchasing replaced it with the approved SKU on inv-640. Use the supplied stable ID; do not search by title or touch “Winter Deicer Pallet Reorder — Approved SKU” (inv-640) or the similarly named record at “South Depot”. If the cancellation times out or otherwise leaves commit state uncertain, do not issue a second cancellation: retrieve inv-277 first. If that get verifies cancelled, stop. Do not list records, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, or notify. Use exactly two bullets: verification result first, retry decision second.
